transcript_text:
spk_0: Okay, perfect. So... okay, let's just begin then. So, tell me a bit about yourself. Like, what are you currently doing? What year are you in? And... yeah, okay.
spk_1: Yeah, I am. So, I'm a first-year at the University of Bath. I'm studying international management, and I'm going to be doing my placement for third year. Yeah, I think I... I'm trying to think of, like, some of my modules, like doing, like, business analytics, marketing, and things like that. Do you want to know about, like, my hobbies as well? Is that good?
spk_0: Yeah, yeah, just like... tell me about, like, your contact hours. So, how much... like, how many hours is, like... like lectures, like, per week? And how much, like, independent study? And also just your timetable structure... and, like, if you're in any extracurriculars as well, that would be great!
spk_1: Okay. Yeah, so I... per day, my lectures range from about... it can be anywhere from an hour to, I think, six hours is the max per day. So it's usually probably in the middle. And then, in terms of extracurriculars, I do volleyball, so that is also kind of in my schedule as well. So, two times a week, I have two-hour practices. So that kind of takes up some time as well. And then, I don't really do any clubs or societies, but I would say, for the most part, my week is taken up by the time of lectures, and I have to obviously plan a lot around that.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah, okay. No, I do management with marketing as well. Like, I'm on my placement right now. The vibe of the timetable... some days, Mondays, it'd be like seven hours, and then some... like one lecture or no lecture?
spk_1: No, it's... it's actually crazy. Yeah, the difference.
spk_0: I can see that you're in... quads.
spk_1: Yes, I am... Can you tell by the bright green?
spk_0: Bright green... I was just gonna ask... So, I forgot how many people, like, is like in that, like, poll.
spk_1: So, there's 10 people per flat.
spk_0: Okay.
spk_1: I think it's like anywhere from 10 to 12 people per flat.
spk_0: Yeah, yeah... 10, including you?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay. I was just gonna ask, do you make, like, who makes the food decisions? Obviously, it would be probably you, like, in your flat. But, like, do you, like, cook together with, like, your flatmates or, like, do grocery shopping together maybe, or...?
spk_1: Yeah, sometimes... So what we've done more often recently is doing food orders together. It kind of saves some time from going into town and doing a food shop that way. So we'll... if it's from, like, Sainsbury's or Tesco's, we'll all have, like, one computer, and then one person will do the big order, and then we'll separate the cost. Usually, we'll do that with, like, three people max per order. But other than that, I use, like, online food orders, like, probably weekly. I think it's so much more convenient!
spk_0: And... yeah, okay, amazing! We're gonna get all into that. I have some more questions later, so I'll keep a note of that. Now, I just wanted to ask you stuff about, like, social media. What social platforms, like, do you use? Like TikTok, Instagram, Snap, X?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah... So, I use... typically use TikTok, Snapchat, and Instagram. Those are like the main ones. Yeah.
spk_0: Which ones do you use, like... the most?
spk_1: I would say I use, like, TikTok and Instagram the most... Yeah!
spk_0: So, if you had to put it, like, in an order... would it be?
spk_1: It would probably be... I feel like Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat.
spk_0: Okay... do you use that, like, any YouTube or, like, Facebook or, like, X?
spk_1: Not particularly... no.
spk_0: Okay, and roughly, like, how much time do you spend on them, like, per day? Like, screen time?
spk_1: I would say I probably spend... it really just depends on the day. But I could... it could be anywhere from, like, two hours a day to, like... it's, like, embarrassing, but five hours a day. Like anywhere between that... not embarrassing at all.
spk_0: Sometimes, I'm just... zoom-scrolling through anything.
spk_1: Literally. Literally.
spk_0: I'll do it. Do you see any, like, food-related content, like, online?
spk_1: Yeah, so I see food-related content a lot on Instagram. So, I see a lot of, like, advertisements related to that. And that's how I kind of got into doing, like, online food orders more than just doing, like, in person... because I saw a lot of people doing it, and I was like, oh, that's, like, so much easier! I may as well do that instead. Yeah.
spk_0: Do you follow any, like, food influencers or, like, creators or, like, food... food accounts, even, like, local maybe?
spk_1: Yeah, I follow a few for, kind of like, healthy... like, recipe ideas for, like, dinner and lunch. Yeah, so I usually use it for, like, recipe inspiration and stuff like that.
spk_0: Yeah, I do the same. I have, like, a TikTok... yeah, like, folder. Have you ever ordered food because you saw it on social media, like something being promoted? Because you talked about, obviously, doing online, like, food deliveries or, like, grocery shops from, like, seeing stuff on Instagram.
spk_1: Yeah, yeah, so I've seen. I mean, there's a few places where I, like, consistently order from, so... like grocery stores. But if I see something that, like... like, for dinner, like, if I want to order out, like, at some point, then it will. Usually, I feel like I'll kind of get, like, reminded, like, oh, I can just order dinner out, like, if I see it pop up on Instagram or TikTok.
spk_0: Okay, nice. Was it like... if you would order it... was it like the food itself that was enticing you? Or like, was it an offer, or like the creator maybe... it would?
spk_1: So, I feel like if I have an offer on it, I would be much more incentivized to just buy it because it's like... may as well use it, and it's cheaper. But if I saw something like a new product or, like, a new food that they had, then maybe that will, like, entice me to buy it as well.
spk_0: Can you walk me through, like, a typical weekday from morning to night? Like, what do you eat, when, and where?
spk_1: Yeah, so if... okay, so like a Monday, for example, I would get up, go to my morning lectures. I don't... I'm not a big breakfast person, so I don't really eat breakfast. But then, um, around lunchtime, I would eat like a little something, like probably like a wrap or like yogurt. And then, at that point, I would need to do my food shop because I have no food, because I ate everything from the week before. And then it would usually come within the hour or two hours. And then after, I would go to my afternoon lectures. I would come back, go to the gym, then I would make dinner with the food that I ordered and, yeah, eat dinner and then usually go to bed right after that.
spk_0: Is that, like, typical throughout the week? Would that be, like, the usual schedule?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah... it would usually be like that.
spk_0: Okay, and does it differ on, like, weekends?
spk_1: Yeah, so weekends I have, like, a lot more flexibility. So I feel like with having more flexibility, sometimes I'll be going into town during the weekends and maybe getting food there, like if I wanted to. I don't do this, like, typically, but if I happen to be in town, then I would get something a little bit more interesting for dinner. Like if it was, like, a Saturday, I would go to, like, Marks and Spencer's and get something to bring back home if I had, like, no food and then... Yeah. But weekends, I feel like I have a lot more time to plan my meals because I don't have to work around lectures. Yeah. So, like, lectures make it more difficult. Okay.
spk_0: Do you cook, like, often?
spk_1: Yeah, so I usually cook. I try and do two nights where I meal prep, like, for the entire week. So, like, I'll meal prep on Monday; then that will have me food for Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday... and then I'll meal prep on Thursday, and then that's the food for the rest of the week.
spk_0: What does cooking usually mean for you? Is it like something from scratch? Like something prepared... prepare, pre-prepared that you just, like, heat up? But yeah... what does, like, cooking generally mean to you?
spk_1: So, I generally cook everything from scratch, or at least I try to be a little bit more health-conscious. So, I'll usually try and get, you know, every single component: like I get my protein, my carbs, and my vegetables. And if it's not vegetables in that meal, then I'll have fruit in place of that to eat after. But yeah, everything that I try and make is like... I make it all myself rather than having freezer food or pre-packaged food. I think it's just like... it makes me feel better about the food choices that I'm making.
spk_0: Amazing! How often do you order, like, food or maybe even grocery delivery?
spk_1: I feel like I don't order food out a lot, but in terms of groceries, I'm probably ordering at least once or twice a week.
spk_0: Once a week... Is this like separate from the group order? Would that just be the biggest group order that you do?
spk_1: So that would be... if we're going to do a group... that's... if we're gonna do a group order, then that would count as my, like, once per week, because that would have all my groceries.
spk_0: Okay, so would it either be like a big group order, or would you order it by yourself... like, alone? Yes.
spk_1: Yeah... so, it would be either-or.
spk_0: Which meals feel the most stressful, and which meals feel the most enjoyable?
spk_1: I feel like the most stressful meals are definitely dinner. It is very time-consuming to make dinner because, with lunch, it can be something a bit more simple, like... just like a Greek yogurt bowl or something like that. But for dinner, I'm trying to get, like, everything in. So, for example, last night I think I spent, like, an hour and... like almost an hour and a half making dinner. And that's, like, stressful because I'm like, I could be getting work done right, right now! And I was like, this is pushing back my sleep as well. So that's definitely the most stressful meal of the week. Okay.
spk_0: And which meals feel the most, like... enjoyable?
spk_1: I think lunch, because I can just easily, like... it's like a 15-minute thing, and I can eat with my friends.
spk_0: Okay... yeah. When do you think food feels functional, social, and comforting?
spk_1: I feel like it's when we are all in the kitchen at the same time, making food, and we have the ability to, like, eat dinner all together or even, like, going out for dinner... like, that's such a great thing, and I really enjoy that! And it's definitely comforting as well. It kind of reminds me of, like, being at home, and I really... that's something I really enjoy doing. But on, like, a weekly basis, just kind of being in the kitchen at the same time and all cooking together... it's really great.
spk_0: Roughly, how much do you spend on food... like, in a typical week?
spk_1: I would spend, usually, anywhere from £25 to £45 on a typical week.
spk_0: Is that including, like, food delivery, groceries, and also eating out?
spk_1: That's just including... I would say that's just including the groceries delivery. Any other expenses that I do on food will be with, like, my, you know, bath go, right?
spk_0: Yeah... because you get quads, right?
spk_1: Yeah. So, I try and use that to the best of my ability. Like, if I don't have time to make lunch, I'll try and use my Bathgo credit or buy something from the market that I can use to make dinner.
spk_0: Okay, so, like, per week, how much do you think you spend, like, roughly, overall, and how much would that go into, like, food delivery or groceries or, like, eating out?
spk_1: I would say, typically during a week, I'm probably spending around $55 to $65 in total on just food alone. It really just depends on the week and what I have in my cupboard already... but I try and stay within that range.
spk_0: Is that like a typical, like, budget? Do you, like, firm to that or, like, does it vary? Like, I really...
spk_1: So, in terms of my grocery deliveries, like, I really try and stay within that 25 to 45 range... Like, 45 being, like, the absolute max. And then for... It gets hard to kind of count everything when I start buying things on campus as well. So that's why it's kind of hard to pinpoint, like, because sometimes I'll be like, oh, I, like, want a snack or something. And then it's like, if you're getting a snack every single day, that's like another, like, 15 quid added to the cost. So I would say probably, like, a safe estimate is probably 40... Anywhere from 45 to 60. Yeah!
spk_0: This quads card... trust me, I know I didn't use to live in quads, but my, like, friends did, and they were just like, 'free money.' And then it was.
spk_1: It's honestly so dangerous. It does feel like, 'Oh, I'm not spending anything,' but you are. You definitely are. Yeah.
spk_0: So, you were talking about how you don't really order food out as much... right? Unless it's like going out with your friends, you order mostly, like, groceries, or you would, I guess, get food out technically from, like, campus with the food credit.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: But usually... like, what reason do you normally order food? Delivery?
spk_1: Other than groceries...?
spk_0: Yeah... other than groceries.
spk_1: I feel like the only... I would order food out if I had an offer, or if it was like a special occasion, or maybe if it was the weekend. I feel like those are like the three... I feel like the main reason is if I did have a discount. If I had a discount on food, then I would probably try and use that discount before its expiry date. But then another case would be like if I didn't feel like cooking, or if it was kind of the weekend, so it's kind of like a little bit more chill. I think those are probably the cases that I would use.
spk_0: So, would you say, like, the day of the week that you would most likely order food... would that be on weekends, or maybe sometime during the week where it's, like, busy, or what do you like?
spk_1: I would... I would probably say, if it's not groceries, I would probably order food Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
spk_0: Okay... does delivery feel like a treat, a habit, or just a part of your normal spending?

spk_0: Okay, so... this was a grocery shop?

spk_1: Yeah, so it would be every week that I do, like, the weekly food shop. And I've actually... I think the week before last, I think I actually got a voucher from Deliveroo. spk_0: Okay, and how much is price taken into consideration when you shop for groceries?

spk_0: out

spk_0: Okay, when do you decide not to order for delivery? And, like, sometimes... like, what makes... what makes delivery feel worth it? For example,
spk_1: I feel like delivery makes it feel worth it when it's quite timely. So when it, like, comes to my door quite fast, um, I feel like it... For certain food delivery services, where it comes within the period of, like, 24 hours, or it might come, like, at any time within the next day, that can be kind of difficult to plan everything out. spk_0: Okay, what about on offers and promotions for both Uber Eats and Deliver... what would you rate out of five?

spk_1: I think... for both Uber Eats and Delivery.

I feel like. So that's where I'm, like, willing to even take, like, 1 or 2 pounds off and switch to the other kind. But I think the thing that is more important for me is certain things, like cosmetics and, like, certain, like, bath and body type of products, where I need, like, a specific kind of those things.
